title: Implement scheduler with work-stealing run queues
purpose: The scheduler is what makes fairness real. Without it, processes are data structures with no heartbeat. This brief delivers the scheduler thread pool (one thread per core by default), per-thread run queues, the schedule-execute-yield loop, work stealing (idle threads take half from the busiest queue), and clean shutdown. No async runtime in the hot path — plain OS threads plus lock-free queues. This is the piece that turns 'we have processes' into 'processes actually run fairly.'
task: Implement the scheduler in crates/beamr/src/scheduler/. The Scheduler creates N OS threads (default: available_parallelism). Each thread owns a RunQueue (lock-free deque). The main loop: pop a process from the run queue, set its status to Running, call the interpreter for one time slice (reduction budget), handle the result (Yielded → push back to run queue, Waiting → move to wait set, Exited → run exit handlers + remove). Work stealing: when a thread's queue is empty, it tries to steal half the processes from a random other thread's queue. Implement Scheduler::spawn(entry_module, entry_function, args) to create and enqueue a new process. Implement Scheduler::shutdown() for clean termination.

requirements.title: Scheduler thread pool
requirements.spec: beamr::scheduler SHALL define a Scheduler struct that spawns N OS threads where N defaults to std::thread::available_parallelism(). Each thread runs the scheduler loop. Scheduler::new(config) accepts a SchedulerConfig with thread_count override. Threads SHALL be named 'beamr-sched-N' for debuggability.
acceptance: Scheduler::new(default_config) creates threads equal to available_parallelism()
Scheduler::new(config with thread_count=4) creates exactly 4 threads
Threads are named beamr-sched-0, beamr-sched-1, etc.
Scheduler::shutdown() joins all threads cleanly

requirements.title: Per-thread run queue
requirements.spec: beamr::scheduler::run_queue SHALL define a RunQueue backed by a lock-free deque (crossbeam-deque). The owning thread pushes and pops from the back (LIFO for cache locality). Stealers pop from the front (FIFO for fairness). RunQueue::push(process) adds a process. RunQueue::pop() removes and returns a process for execution. RunQueue::steal_half(target) takes approximately half the processes from target's queue.
acceptance: push then pop returns the same process
Stealing from a queue with 10 processes takes approximately 5
Stealing from an empty queue returns nothing (no error, no panic)
Push and steal from different threads do not race

requirements.title: Schedule-execute-yield loop
requirements.spec: EACH scheduler thread SHALL run a loop: (1) pop a process from its run queue (or steal if empty), (2) set process status to Running, (3) call the interpreter execute function for one time slice, (4) handle the result — Yielded: reset reductions, push back to run queue; Waiting: move to the global wait set; Exited: run cleanup. WHEN no process is available (own queue empty, steal fails) THE THREAD SHALL park briefly then retry.
acceptance: A spawned process runs to completion
A process that exhausts its reduction budget yields and re-runs later
A process that enters receive with no messages transitions to Waiting
An exited process is removed from the process table
An idle thread does not spin-loop at 100% CPU (it parks)

requirements.title: Work stealing
requirements.spec: WHEN a scheduler thread's run queue is empty THE SYSTEM SHALL attempt to steal work from another thread's queue. The victim is selected by round-robin (not random — deterministic for testing). Stealing takes approximately half the victim's processes. IF all queues are empty THE SYSTEM SHALL park the thread for a short duration before retrying. Work stealing SHALL NOT starve any single queue — a queue with only one process SHALL NOT be stolen from.
acceptance: A thread with an empty queue steals from a thread with processes
Stolen processes execute on the stealing thread
A queue with 1 process is not stolen from (the owning thread keeps it)
Round-robin victim selection visits all threads before repeating

requirements.title: Process spawn via scheduler
requirements.spec: Scheduler::spawn(module: Atom, function: Atom, arity: u8, args: &[Term]) SHALL create a new Process, set its entry point to the module:function/arity code pointer, load args into X registers, assign a PID from the process table, and push it onto a run queue (round-robin across threads for initial placement). It SHALL return the PID.
acceptance: spawn returns a valid PID
The spawned process eventually executes (observable via side effects or BIF calls)
Args are loaded into X registers 0..N-1
Multiple spawns distribute across run queues

requirements.title: Clean shutdown
requirements.spec: Scheduler::shutdown() SHALL signal all scheduler threads to stop, wait for in-progress processes to complete their current time slice, then join all threads. Processes still in run queues or wait sets at shutdown are abandoned (not executed). Shutdown SHALL complete in bounded time — it does not wait for blocked processes.
acceptance: shutdown() returns after all threads have joined
A process mid-execution finishes its current time slice before the thread exits
shutdown() does not hang on a process in Waiting status
Calling shutdown() twice does not panic

requirements.title: Scheduler tests
requirements.spec: WHEN the scheduler is tested THE SYSTEM SHALL include tests for: single-process execution to completion, multiple processes with fair scheduling, work stealing under load imbalance, spawn distributes across queues, clean shutdown, and idle parking (no CPU spin).
acceptance: cargo test -p beamr scheduler passes with all tests green
Tests cover: single process runs, multi-process fairness (all N processes complete), work stealing moves processes, shutdown completes

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement priority scheduling — all processes share the same priority (add later if needed)
SHALL NOT implement dirty schedulers — separate brief (B-013 scope)
SHALL NOT implement the interpreter execute function — the scheduler calls it, but the interpreter brief implements it
SHALL NOT implement timer-based wake-ups for Waiting processes — timer brief scope
SHALL NOT implement any async/Tokio in the scheduler hot path — plain OS threads only (D3)
verification: cargo test -p beamr scheduler passes with all tests green
Code inspection: no tokio or async in scheduler code
Code inspection: threads are plain std::thread::spawn, not tokio tasks
Code inspection: run queue uses crossbeam-deque or equivalent lock-free structure
